requirementGathering: Business Objectives: Clearly define the business value and objectives
User Stories: Document user stories with acceptance criteria
Functional Requirements: Specify what the feature should accomplish
Non-Functional Requirements: Define performance, security, and scalability needs
Constraints: Identify technical, budget, and timeline constraints
requirementsAnalysis.scopeDefinition:
Define Clearly:
- What is included in this enhancement
- What is explicitly excluded from scope
- Dependencies on other features or systems
- Integration points with existing functionality
- Migration requirements for existing data/users
impactAssessment: Affected Components: Identify all system components that will be modified
Integration Points: Map all systems that will be impacted
User Impact: Assess how changes will affect different user types
Performance Impact: Evaluate potential performance implications
Security Implications: Identify security considerations and requirements

architectureDesign: System Architecture: Follow established architectural patterns and principles
Component Design: Design components with single responsibility
Interface Design: Define clear, consistent APIs and interfaces
Data Model: Design efficient and scalable data structures
Integration Strategy: Plan integration with existing systems
designPhase.designPrinciples:
Follow Enterprise Principles:
- SOLID principles for object-oriented design
- DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) for code reusability
- KISS (Keep It Simple, Stupid) for maintainability
- YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It) for scope management
- Separation of concerns for modularity
scalabilityAndPerformance: Performance Requirements: Define specific performance targets
Scalability Planning: Design for expected growth and load
Caching Strategy: Plan caching at appropriate layers
Database Design: Optimize database schema and queries
Resource Management: Plan for efficient resource utilization
securityDesign: Data Protection: Plan encryption and data protection measures
Input Validation: Design comprehensive input validation
Audit Logging: Plan audit trail and compliance requirements

developmentApproach: Incremental Development: Break enhancement into manageable chunks
Feature Flags: Use feature flags for gradual rollout
API Versioning: Maintain backward compatibility with versioning
Database Migrations: Plan and test database changes
implementationStandards.codeQualityStandards:
Implementation Requirements:
- Follow established coding standards and conventions
- Implement comprehensive error handling and logging
- Write self-documenting code with appropriate comments
- Create modular, reusable components
- Maintain consistent naming and structure
testingStrategy: Unit Testing: Test individual components and functions
End-to-End Testing: Test complete user workflows
Security Testing: Test security measures and vulnerabilities
